evt_022.label: Drop
evt_022.basis: Party D stopped pursuing the transaction after the Company refused to commit that it would not enter into an agreement with another bidder during Party D's requested diligence period.
evt_022.source_text: In light of the active interest of Party B and G& W, the Company declined to make such a commitment, at which point Party D indicated that it would not proceed with further due diligence at that time.

initiation_judgment.basis: Party A's unsolicited expression of acquisition interest prompted the Board to hire an advisor and start a sale process.
initiation_judgment.source_text: Upon receiving a report of this meeting, in recognition of its public company obligations, the Board appointed a subcommittee of two independent directors, Frank W. Barrett and Richard W. Anderson, to interview potential investment bankers to represent the Company in possible negotiations with Party A and to assist the Board in evaluating additional strategic options for the realization of shareholder value, including potential change in control transactions.

advisor_bmo_capital_markets.source_text: Following this presentation, the Board approved the subcommittee's recommendation and the Company engaged GHF.

advisor_hinckley_allen.source_text: Members of management and representatives of each of GHF and the Company's legal counsel, Hinckley, Allen & Snyder LLP (which we refer to as "Hinckley Allen"), participated in all meetings of the Transaction Committee, except as otherwise indicated.

advisor_simpson_thacher.source_text: On August 10, 2016, representatives of Hinckley Allen and Simpson Thacher & Bartlett LLP (which we refer to as "Simpson Thacher"), G& W's legal counsel, discussed various provisions of the draft merger agreement and voting agreement that had been provided to G& W on August 3, 2016.

count_reconciliation.note: The extract names five strategic buyers tied to the original outreach cohort (Party A, G& W, Party B, Party E, and Party F); the filing's total of 11 includes six unnamed strategic buyers, and Party C joined later outside that March outreach.
Only Party D is individually identified as a financial bidder in the extract; the other contacted financial buyers are unnamed in the filing.

count_reconciliation.assertion: potential strategic buyers that executed confidentiality agreements
count_reconciliation.note: The extract has explicit NDA events for Party A, G& W, and Party B from the original contacted strategic cohort; the remaining strategic NDA signers are unnamed, and Party C signed later as a separate entrant.

count_reconciliation.assertion: potential financial buyers that executed confidentiality agreements
count_reconciliation.note: The filing gives only an aggregate count of 14 financial NDA signers; no financial bidder NDA event is individually extracted.
The May 19 to June 1, 2016 IOI round is reported only in aggregate, so no bidder-specific event from that nine-IOI set is individually extracted; Party C's July 12 IOI falls outside this assertion.

count_reconciliation.assertion: potential buyers advanced to management presentations and additional due diligence
count_reconciliation.note: Five later-named bidders can be traced to this stage (G& W, Party B, Party D, Party E, and Party F); two additional advancing bidders are unnamed, and Party C joined later outside the original seven.

count_reconciliation.assertion: letters of intent received in late July 2016
count_reconciliation.note: Counts match exactly: the extract has six late-July LOI proposal events for G& W, Party B, Party C, Party D, Party E, and Party F.

count_reconciliation.assertion: buyers that declined to submit a late-July LOI
count_reconciliation.note: The filing states that one strategic buyer and one financial buyer declined to submit an LOI, but neither bidder is individually identified in the extract.

count_reconciliation.assertion: low bidders excluded after the IOI round
count_reconciliation.note: The two low bidders excluded after the IOI round are described only in aggregate and are not individually identifiable in the extract.
